description: exit with error:false (early return), the complement to OG-EX-07's error:true. The event reaching the exit node is emitted to the graph's output stream and the graph invocation then terminates immediately; all still-in-flight events are discarded. Pins the early-return clause of 'Node definitions / exit' and the determinism rule that the output includes any event emitted by an exit with error:false. The exit node has no outgoing edge (OG-V-16): it emits to the output stream directly, not through the output node, which stays reachable (OG-V-06) via the item path through a buffer whose partial contents the exit discards.

fixtures.name: exit error:false emits the event then terminates, discarding in-flight events
fixtures.section: Node definitions / exit; Determinism and portability
fixtures.description: unpack emits a cancel sentinel and two items. The sentinel matches the cancel filter and reaches the exit (error:false), which emits it to the output stream and terminates the graph. The two items pass the item filter into a buffer, which only flushes at normal completion, so the exit's termination discards the buffered items before any reaches the output node. The output is exactly the early-returned sentinel under every interleaving: an exit preempts the end-of-stream flush, so the cross-path race between the cancel and item paths cannot leak an item. This exercises the spec's 'in-flight events (including partial buffer contents) are discarded' clause directly.
